title: Istanbul + Cappadocia in 5 Days
subtitle: The combo most first-time Turkey travelers actually want. Book the balloon flight before anything else — it sells out.
intro: Five days is the sweet spot: 2 nights Istanbul for the icons, 2 nights Cappadocia for the balloon and cave hotels, 1 flex night that you use in whichever side hooks you. Flights between are 80 min and $60–120. Do it in this order — Cappadocia first if the balloon is non-negotiable (gives you a second shot if weather cancels).
preTrip: Book the hot air balloon NOW. 3-month lead time in peak season. Royal Balloon, Butterfly Balloons, and Voyager are the three operators with the best safety records.
Pre-book the internal flight Istanbul → Kayseri (closer to Cappadocia than Nevşehir airport). Turkish Airlines and Pegasus fly 4–6x daily. ~$60–120 one-way.
Activate an Airalo Turkey eSIM before you fly — saves airport-SIM hassle.
Pre-book both airport transfers (IST → Istanbul city, Kayseri/Nevşehir → your Cappadocia cave hotel). Cave hotels are remote; a taxi meter can run $40+.
Reserve your cave hotel 2+ months ahead if you want Museum Hotel, Argos in Cappadocia, Sultan Cave Suites, or Kayakapi. These sell out consistently.

days.title: Day 1 — Arrive Istanbul (Sultanahmet)
days.staybase: Sultanahmet — you want to wake up next to Hagia Sophia tomorrow

flow.what: Pre-booked transfer from IST to Sultanahmet (50 min).
Check in + rest. Don't try to do sights on arrival day — jet lag costs more than you think.
Sunset walk: Sultanahmet Square → Hippodrome → Cistern plaza. Orientation only, no tickets yet.
Dinner at Matbah (Ottoman cuisine inside Ottoman Imperial Hotel) or Deraliye. Early night.

tips: Jet lag tip: don't sleep before 22:00 local. Light 45-min nap max.
If you arrive before noon: swap this with Day 2 morning.

days.title: Day 2 — Istanbul icons

flow.what: Hotel breakfast, then Blue Mosque (free entry, open 08:30).
Hagia Sophia with pre-booked ticket. Allow 90 min.
Basilica Cistern (pre-booked slot). 30 min.
Lunch at Balıkçı Sabahattin (seafood, classic meyhane).
Topkapı Palace + Harem. 3 hours. Don't rush it.
Ferry across Bosphorus — Eminönü → Kadıköy → back. Cheapest spectacular experience in Turkey.
Dinner in Beyoğlu — Mikla (view splurge), Karaköy Lokantası (meyhane), or Meze by Lemon Tree.

tips: Pack light for tomorrow — small bag only. Luggage stays at Istanbul hotel if doing a round-trip, or ships with you.
Tomorrow is an early flight. Set alarms.

days.title: Day 3 — Fly to Cappadocia + first afternoon

flow.what: Early transfer to IST airport. Fly to Kayseri (80 min).
Land + transfer to your cave hotel (~60 min). Check in.
Lunch at Topdeck Cave Restaurant (Göreme) or Seki Restaurant (Uçhisar).
Göreme Open Air Museum — the painted rock churches. 2 hours.
Sunset at Red Valley viewpoint. 15-min walk from parking. Bring a jacket.
Dinner at Seten Restaurant (Göreme) — best fine dining in the region.
EARLY night — tomorrow is a 04:30 wake-up for the balloon.

tips: Confirm balloon pickup with the operator after check-in.
Weather can cancel balloons. If it does, Day 4 becomes backup — that's why we gave it flex.

days.title: Day 4 — Balloon sunrise + Cappadocia deep dive

flow.what: Balloon pickup. Takeoff ~05:45. 60-min flight. Traditional champagne toast on landing.
Back to hotel. Full Turkish breakfast on the terrace — this is a life moment.
Rest / pool hour at your cave hotel.
Lunch at Dibek (Göreme) — cooked-in-clay-pot testi kebab.
Kaymaklı or Derinkuyu underground city. Pick one — they're similar, don't do both.
Pigeon Valley + Uçhisar Castle climb.
Sunset at Lovers Hill or Rose Valley.
Dinner at Ziggy's Cafe (Ürgüp) or Old Greek House for the atmosphere.

tips: If balloon cancelled: operator auto-rebooks you for Day 5 morning. Use Day 4 afternoon normally.
Skip the 'whirling dervish' tourist show in Cappadocia — it's for buses. See the real thing in Konya or Galata.

days.title: Day 5 — Flex + fly home
days.staybase: Back to Istanbul or direct home

flow.what: ATV tour through Love Valley (best morning activity) OR horseback ride OR a pottery class in Avanos.
Lunch somewhere with a terrace view — Seten for refined, Ziggy's for casual.
Transfer to Kayseri/Nevşehir airport. Fly to Istanbul or your next stop.
If back in Istanbul: final dinner in Karaköy. If going home: Istanbul layover means airport hotel for an early flight.

tips: Book a hamam in Cappadocia morning if you want real relaxation — 60-min scrub at Museum Hotel or Kelebek hammam.
If you have an extra day: take the overnight bus to Pamukkale instead of flying home. Turkey's other iconic photo spot, 90% less touristy.

scams: Balloon operator undercutting the known brands. Safety record matters. Stick to Royal Balloon, Butterfly, Voyager, Kapadokya Balloons.
Kayseri airport taxi drivers quoting $100+ for the 60-minute ride. Pre-book transfer: $35–50 fixed.
'Special rug village' tour stops that materialize mid-day trip. Politely refuse.
Fake 'Turkish night' shows advertised in hotel lobbies — 80% are mediocre; if you want the performance, pick a real Sema ceremony in a museum setting.
checklist: Travel insurance active (balloon flying is covered by SafetyWing nomad plan)
